title: Product Designer
description:
About the job
🗓️ Working Days and Hours: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 6 PM (UK Hours); Potential for Flexible Hours
📍 Location: Remote/Online
💷 Salary Range: Attractive, Top-of-Market Salary (paid in GBP/USD)

Palm Outsourcing helps international companies find talent in Egypt.

Please note, we will only be considering excellent applicants with solid demonstrable experience. If you do not have clear and evidenced experience, you will be immediately rejected, so please do not apply.

Contrastingly, if you feel you are a right fit relative to the requirements below, please proceed.

Opportunity Overview
Our client is a British beauty brand renowned for botanically-inspired bath and body products. Experience in the beauty, personal care, or lifestyle sector is important for this role. This is a production-focused opportunity to join a brand that values craft, precision, and visual excellence across its packaging, print, and digital assets.

Role Description
We're looking for an Artworker to join our client's creative team. If you're a detail-oriented production specialist who thrives on delivering pixel-perfect files across packaging, print, and digital formats, this could be the role for you. You'll take design concepts and produce flawless, production-ready artwork with minimal oversight, ensuring every asset meets spec and brand standards. This position offers the chance to work with a prestigious British beauty brand in a fast-paced, craft-driven environment.

Key Responsibilities
Artwork Production – Take design concepts and produce flawless, print-ready and digital-ready files to exact specifications.
Packaging Artwork – Prepare and finalise packaging artwork, ensuring accuracy across dimensions, colour profiles, and bleed requirements.
File Preparation – Build production-ready files in Adobe InDesign, Illustrator, and Photoshop, maintaining clean layer structures and naming conventions.
Quality Control – Proof all artwork meticulously before release, checking for typos, colour accuracy, alignment, and brand consistency.
Digital Asset Production – Adapt and resize creative assets for digital channels, ensuring files meet platform specifications.

Minimum Qualifications
Adobe Creative Suite – Proficiency in InDesign, Illustrator, and Photoshop for production artwork across print and digital formats.
Print Production – Solid understanding of print specifications, prepress requirements, colour profiles, and file preparation standards.
Attention to Detail – Excellent eye for accuracy, consistency, and precision across layouts, typography, and brand assets.
Production Speed – Ability to work quickly and efficiently under tight deadlines without compromising quality.
Communication – Strong written English communication skills for liaising with designers and stakeholders.

Bonus Qualifications
Beauty & FMCG Packaging – Previous experience preparing artwork for beauty, personal care, or FMCG packaging.

Company Benefits
Top-of-the-market pay.
Remote working.
Potential for flexible hours.
Typically, we work with our own laptops, but if you need any extra gadgets to do your best work, we've got you covered.
Becoming a part of Palm Outsourcing, where your performance is rewarded in long-term job security and continued growth opportunities.

Thank you for taking the time to apply. We look forward to reviewing your application.

title: The UI/UX Designer is responsible for designing intuitive, modern, and highly usable digital experiences within an InvestTech / FinTech platform.
link:
About the job
Job Summary
The UI/UX Designer is responsible for designing intuitive, modern, and highly usable digital experiences within an InvestTech / FinTech platform.
This role goes beyond visual design. It requires a strong understanding of:
user behavior
interaction design
engagement mechanics
The designer is expected to maintain a high standard of product taste, ensuring the platform is:
clean and simple
visually modern
easy to navigate
engaging to use
The role works closely with product and engineering to ensure designs are not only visually strong, but also practical, consistent, and implementable.

KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES & ACTIVITIES
1. UX Design & User Flows
Design clear and intuitive user journeys across the platform
Simplify complex financial concepts into usable interfaces
Identify and remove friction points in the user experience

2. UI Design & Visual Quality
Deliver modern, clean, and consistent UI designs
Maintain a high standard of visual quality and product aesthetics
Ensure design consistency across all screens and features

3. Product Collaboration
Work closely with Product Manager to:
understand requirements
translate them into effective design solutions
Align design decisions with product goals and user needs

4. Design Execution
Produce wireframes, mockups, and high-fidelity designs
Use tools such as Figma to create and iterate designs
Ensure designs are ready for development with clear specifications

5. Usability & User-Centric Design
Apply UX principles to improve usability and clarity
Design interfaces that are easy to understand for non-expert users
Ensure users can navigate and interact with the platform without confusion

6. Engagement & Interaction Design
Design interactions that improve:
user engagement
retention
feature adoption
Incorporate gamification or behavioral design elements where relevant

7. Continuous Improvement
Iterate designs based on:
user feedback
product data
team input
Continuously refine the experience to improve usability and engagement

8. Cross-Functional Coordination
Work with engineering to ensure designs are implemented correctly
Provide design clarifications during development
Ensure alignment between design and final product output

COMPETENCIES
Core
UI Design
UX Design
Interaction Design
Wireframing & Prototyping
Design Systems

Tools
Figma (Strong proficiency required)
Prototyping tools
Basic understanding of design handoff processes

Design & Product Thinking
Strong sense of visual taste and attention to detail
Ability to simplify complex interfaces
Understanding of user behavior and usability principles
Ability to design for clarity, not just aesthetics

Engagement & Behavioral Thinking
Understanding of user engagement principles
Familiarity with gamification concepts
Ability to design experiences that encourage repeated usage

Soft
Clear communication
Collaboration with cross-functional teams
Openness to feedback
Attention to detail
Ability to iterate quickly

Languages
English (Advanced Proficiency)
Arabic (Native)

JOB SPECIFICATIONS
Industry / Domain
FinTech / InvestTech / Digital Platforms

Experience
3-7 years of experience in UI/UX design
Experience designing digital products (web or mobile)
Experience working with product and engineering teams

Education
Degree in Design, HCI, or related field (preferred)

KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS
Quality and clarity of user experience
Visual consistency across the product
Reduction in user friction and confusion
Improvement in engagement and feature usage
Accuracy of design implementation
Speed and effectiveness of design iterations

description:
About the job
JOB PORPORPOISE:

To design innovative, visually compelling, and user-centered solutions that enhance product usability and aesthetics, leveraging AI tools to accelerate the design process and delivering experiences tailored to Arabic speaking users through culturally and linguistically accurate design.

KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES:

Functional Key Responsibilities

Lead UX research, journey mapping, and usability testing initiatives, ensuring solutions resonate with native Arabic speaking audiences
Develop wireframes, prototypes, and high fidelity designs with a high standard of visual craft, incorporating AI powered design tools to improve speed and quality
Design complex user flows and scalable design systems that ensure consistency across products
Collaborate closely with Product Managers and Engineers across cross functional product squads
Ensure consistency across all digital touchpoints and drive design quality standards and governance
Conduct usability testing and iterate on designs based on insights; validate design decisions using data and experimentation
Mentor junior designers and provide design direction
Support UX writing and content design efforts to deliver clear, user friendly copy across interfaces (plus)

Striving For Excellence Responsibilities

Ensure compliance with relevant regulations while promoting organizational transparency
Collaborate effectively with colleagues and contribute to shared team objectives
Provide regular updates on performance and document any issues or challenges to ensure timely resolution
Stay current with emerging AI design tools and design system best practices, sharing learnings with the team

Requirements

Bachelor's degree in Design, or a related field
5+ years of experience in Product Design or UX roles
Proven experience designing scalable digital products
Experience collaborating with cross functional product squads
Exceptional visual design craft, with a strong eye for typography, layout, color, and visual hierarchy
Proven understanding and hands on use of AI tools for design
Native Arabic speaker

Preferred:

Experience building and maintaining design systems
UX writing and content design skills
Professional certification from Nielsen Norman Group (NN/g) or IDEO U

title: 📌 Position: Senior Product UI/UX Designer
link:
About the job
📢 Please read the job description carefully and ensure your CV is fully updated 📢

📌 Position: Senior Product UI/UX Designer
📍 Location: 100% Remote – Based in Egypt
🕘 Job Type: Full-time contract
📅 Contract Duration: 6 months
💰 Salary: Competitive salary (based on experience)
🎓 Experience: 5+ years of experience in UI/UX and product design
🚀 Start Date: Immediate
💼 Industry: Telecommunications / Digital Products / Consumer Technology
📅 Working Days: From Sunday to Thursday

🔵 About the Role
We are looking for a Senior Product UI/UX Designer to lead the design of intuitive, scalable, and user-centered digital experiences across mobile and web products.
This role is ideal for a designer who combines strong product thinking, solid UX problem-solving, and high-quality UI execution. The ideal candidate should have experience designing customer-facing digital products, simplifying complex user journeys, and collaborating closely with product, engineering, and business stakeholders.
The role requires someone who can deeply understand user needs, translate business requirements into clear design solutions, and maintain consistency across journeys, components, and platforms.

🔵 Key Responsibilities
Lead end-to-end UI/UX design for web and mobile product experiences
Translate business requirements and product goals into user flows, wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity UI designs
Improve complex user journeys and make them intuitive, seamless, and conversion-friendly
Create scalable, consistent, and reusable design systems and component libraries
Work closely with product managers, developers, QA, and stakeholders to deliver production-ready experiences
Conduct user journey analysis, usability reviews, and design improvements based on feedback and product goals
Design responsive interfaces for web and mobile environments
Ensure high attention to visual hierarchy, clarity, accessibility, and usability
Maintain consistency across Arabic and English experiences, including RTL and LTR layouts when needed
Support product discovery, feature design, design reviews, and design handoff to engineering teams
Continuously refine and improve existing product experiences based on user behavior and business priorities

🔵 Required Qualifications
5+ years of experience in UI/UX design, product design, or a similar role
Strong portfolio demonstrating mobile and web product design
Strong experience in user flows, wireframing, prototyping, and high-fidelity UI design
Strong command of Figma
Experience designing customer-facing digital products
Strong understanding of usability principles, interaction design, and information architecture
Ability to simplify complex workflows into clear and elegant user experiences
Experience creating and maintaining design systems
Strong collaboration skills with product and engineering teams
Ability to clearly explain design decisions and rationale
Strong sense of visual design, spacing, hierarchy, and modern interface patterns

🔵 Preferred Qualifications
Experience in telecommunications, consumer apps, fintech, e-commerce, or other high-usage digital products
Experience designing for self-service digital journeys
Experience with RTL/LTR interfaces and bilingual digital products
Familiarity with accessibility standards and responsive design best practices
Experience using product or behavior data to inform design decisions
Experience working in fast-paced product teams with multiple stakeholders
Familiarity with developer handoff, design QA, and implementation follow-up

🔵 What We’re Looking For
A strong product-minded designer, not just a visual designer
Someone who can improve user experience, not only create beautiful screens
A designer who understands business goals and user pain points equally well
Someone who can bring structure, clarity, and consistency to complex digital products
A collaborative team player with strong ownership and attention to detail

🔵 Why Join Fekra?
🏆 ISO-Certified & ISTQB Platinum Partner – Be part of a globally recognized company.
🌍 100% Remote – Work from anywhere!
💰 Competitive Salary
🚀 Exciting Projects – Work on cutting-edge fintech and cloud-based applications.
📈 Career Growth – Enjoy continuous learning and professional development.
💡 Flexible Work Options – Part-time and full-time remote opportunities.
🤝 Collaborative Culture – Join a dynamic, supportive, and innovative team.

Job Description

Zealous is a UK-based submissions management platform used by 500+ arts organisations and 180,000+ creators worldwide. Our clients include the Saatchi Gallery, the British Library, and Cass Arts. We handle the end-to-end process for awards, open calls, grants, residencies, and art fairs - all in one place. We've been building for 15 years and we're a team of fourteen.

 

This is not a short-term project - if successful you will be a core member of our team.

 

We're hiring our first dedicated Product Designer. Until now, design at Zealous has meant UI execution - implementing specs, building components, keeping things consistent. What we haven't had is someone who takes a brief, shapes the flow, prototypes how it should work, and partners with engineering to ship it well. That's this role.

 

You'll work directly with our UK-based Founder and PM, who continue shaping product direction and writing briefs. You'll turn those briefs into the designs we build, and work closely with our frontend lead on handoff. Over time - as you build context - we'd like you to grow into user research, building out our design system, and shaping product thinking earlier. You'll be the only designer in the room - your decisions ship directly, without hundreds of layers of review. That's a growth path, not a day-one expectation.

 

How we work
Fully remote, Sunday–Thursday. Core hours are 9–5 Egypt time but we're genuinely flexible - several of us work around young kids, and we care about the work getting done well, not clock-watching.

 

Day to day responsibilities
Take briefs from the PM and Founder and turn them into well-structured user flows, wireframes, and interactive Figma prototypes
Work closely with our frontend lead on handoff - communicate design intent clearly, answer questions during build, and review what ships against what was designed
Keep our Figma workspace organised and maintainable as it grows - consistent components, sensible naming, files others can find their way around
Sit in design review alongside our frontend lead, flagging feasibility issues and edge cases before things are committed
Raise questions early when a brief is unclear or something doesn't add up - we'd rather hear it upfront than discover it in engineering
 

Growth path over 12–18 months:

Run user research and testing sessions directly with organisers, artists, and judges
Build out a lightweight design system our UI developers can work from without asking
Shape product thinking earlier - helping us decide what to build, not just how it should look
 

The Offer
£1,300–£1,400/month paid in GBP - stable against local currency fluctuation
Health insurance contribution
21 days paid holiday plus Egyptian public holidays
Work within an Egypt-based team but to the standards of a UK product company
Direct access to the Founder, the PM, and the product roadmap
A clear growth path as you take on more ownership
Work that reaches 500+ arts organisations and 180,000 creators worldwide
 

Practical details:

Direct contractor engagement. You'll handle your own tax and social insurance - salary accounts for it, and the team can share their experiences
Use your own laptop to start; we'll provide a company machine after three months
Application to offer within one month
 

Job Requirements

Must-haves:

3+ years of Product Design or UI/UX Design experience - ideally some time on a SaaS product with more than one type of user
A portfolio showing clean, thoughtful Figma work and shipped product - we care more about clarity of thinking than polish of pixels
Strong Figma craft - components, auto-layout, interactive prototypes, tidy files
Clear written and verbal English - you'll be reporting to a UK-based Founder and most of our collaboration happens async in writing
Comfort working directly with engineers and understanding when a design decision creates a build problem
Self-management - you can run your own time and priorities without being chased
Design for how things work, not just how they look
Willing to raise problems early rather than solve around them quietly
 

Bonus points:

Experience running user research or testing (Maze, Useberry, Lookback or similar)
Familiarity with accessibility standards (WCAG)
Exposure to complex form-heavy or multi-role workflows - where getting the logic right matters as much as the layout
A view on where AI tooling is changing design practice, and where it isn't
Care about the creative sector we serve
